title: elaeocarpus serratus
intro: Elaeocarpus serratus, also known as the Blue Duck Berry or Mountain Apple, is a plant species native to eastern Australia and New South Wales. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant's berries, leaves, and bark are rich in antioxidants and have been found to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting immune function. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is often used as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is also used in Ayurvedic and Unani medicine to treat various ailments.
